mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor demand and impending economic contraction, causing investors to price in lower future earnings and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in housing construction and consumer credit expansion reflects robust aggregate demand, which supports corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive SPX returns in at least 50% of out-of-sample windows spanning 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial production coinciding with elevated consumer price inflation indicates supply-side bottlenecks and stagflationary pressures, which compress equity risk premiums and generate negative S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below 99.0 while CPIAUCSL exceeds 235.0
failure_condition: Rank IC between the joint indicator and forward SPX returns remains below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth expands the corporate earnings base, which mechanically supports higher equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a weekly horizon as momentum strategies capture the trend.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly positive when GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.5% annualized over consecutive quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above the central bank target forces prolonged restrictive monetary policy, which increases borrowing costs and suppresses equity multiples, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: Equity forward returns do not decline systematically when inflation remains elevated and policy rates stay above 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects a resilient labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate profitability, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward correlation between PAYEMS month-over-month changes and 60-day SPX returns falls below 0.10 over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where housing starts decline while consumer credit expands signals a shift from durable goods spending to services consumption, which historically precedes equity market corrections due to margin compression in manufacturing-heavy indices.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls > 2% month-over-month while RSAFS rises > 1% month-over-month
failure_condition: The divergence signal fails to predict negative SPX returns in more than 40% of out-of-sample instances over a 3-year rolling window.
